rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical 1,4-alkoxydimerization of aryl alkenes with aliphatic alcohols to afford symmetric and asymmetric 1,4-dialkoxybutanes
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of the styrene to a radical cation; cathodic reduction of protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: distonic benzyl radical cation from radical addition to second styrene (II)
methoxy-substituted carbon radical (III) after MeOH addition
benzyl carbocation (IV) after second anodic oxidation

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes styrene to a radical cation (I), which adds to a second styrene giving distonic radical cation II; methanol traps the cation to give carbon radical III, which is further oxidized at the anode to benzyl cation IV; a second methanol addition affords the 1,4-dialkoxybutane while H+ is reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction completely suppressed with 2 equiv BHT; HRMS detected BHT-adduct V capturing radical intermediate III, confirming radical involvement

probe_reagents.name: Styrene (cyclic voltammetry probe)
probe_reagents.observation: Onset oxidation potential ~+1.713 V vs reference, supporting feasibility of anodic SET to form a cationic carbon species

probe_reagents.name: 1-cyclopropylvinylbenzene (radical clock)
probe_reagents.observation: No cyclopropane ring opening observed; instead 1,2-dimethoxylation byproduct A formed (53%), suggesting the radical is oxidized to a cation before ring opening can occur

notes: Heteroaryl alkenes bearing pyrrole, pyridine, furan (1a-3a) failed, giving complex byproducts. With electron-rich heteroaryl substrate 3a, a 1,2-dimethoxylation side product (B) was observed. Unsuccessful nucleophiles include aniline, benzotriazole, AcOH, TMSCN, and NH4CN. Cross-1,4-alkoxydimerization between two different styrenes is feasible but lower yielding due to homo-dimerization competition.
